name: atlassianUserInfo
description: Get current user info

name: getAccessibleAtlassianResources
description: Get cloudId to make tool calls. When a link is provided (e.g. https://site.atlassian.net/*), try passing the site hostname (e.g. site.atlassian.net) as cloudId to other tools first; if that fails, use this tool to list accessible resources.

name: getConfluencePage
title: Retrieve Confluence page
description: Get a Confluence page or blog post by ID, including body content.

pageId.description: Page or blog post ID, or a Confluence tiny link ID (the encoded part from /wiki/x/ URLs, e.g., Fc1bBw)

contentType.description: Type of content: page or blog post

contentFormat.description: Content format for page/comment body. Use "html" to write or read Confluence content as structured HTML. HTML supports standard blocks plus Confluence HTML+ data-type nodes: panels, status, task/decision lists, expands, layouts, cards, media, dates, mentions, macros/extensions, and sync blocks. HTML is round-trip safe and preserves inline comments and existing local IDs. It must follow valid ADF nesting rules: inline-only task/decision items, headings, and captions; no self-nesting panels, expands, tables, or blockquotes. Invalid HTML will be rejected with a descriptive error so you can retry. Use "markdown" for simplified plain text. Defaults vary by tool when omitted.

name: searchConfluenceUsingCql
title: Search Confluence with CQL
description: Search Confluence content (pages, blog posts, comments, attachments) using CQL (Confluence Query Language). CQL is specific to Confluence and is not interchangeable with JQL.

cql.description: CQL (Confluence Query Language) query string. CQL is specific to Confluence and is not JQL. Common fields: title, text, space, type, creator, contributor, label, ancestor, parent, lastmodified, created, mention, watcher, space.title, space.key. Use space key (not name) for the space field; use space.title ~ "name" for name search. Content types: page, blogpost, comment, attachment. Escape inner quotes with backslash: title ~ "Q2 \"planning\" review". Examples: title ~ "meeting notes" AND type = page | space.title ~ "Engineering" AND lastmodified >= now("-2w") AND type = page | creator = currentUser() AND type = page | text ~ "Q2 \"planning\" review" AND type = page

cqlcontext.description: CQL context for narrowing search scope (e.g., spaceKey)

name: getConfluenceSpaces
description: Get spaces

name: getPagesInConfluenceSpace
description: Get pages or blog posts in a space

contentType.description: Type of content: page or blog post

contentFormat.description: Content format for body content. Use "markdown" for simplified plain text. Use "adf" (Atlassian Document Format, JSON) for full programmatic fidelity. Defaults vary by tool when omitted.

name: getConfluencePageFooterComments
description: Get footer comments for a page or blog post

pageId.description: Page or blog post ID, or a Confluence tiny link ID (the encoded part from /wiki/x/ URLs, e.g., Fc1bBw)

contentType.description: Type of content: page or blog post

contentFormat.description: Content format for body content. Use "markdown" for simplified plain text. Use "adf" (Atlassian Document Format, JSON) for full programmatic fidelity. Defaults vary by tool when omitted.

includeReplies.description: When true, also return reply comments

name: getConfluencePageInlineComments
description: Get inline comments for a page or blog post

pageId.description: Page or blog post ID, or a Confluence tiny link ID (the encoded part from /wiki/x/ URLs, e.g., Fc1bBw)

contentType.description: Type of content: page or blog post

contentFormat.description: Content format for body content. Use "markdown" for simplified plain text. Use "adf" (Atlassian Document Format, JSON) for full programmatic fidelity. Defaults vary by tool when omitted.

includeReplies.description: When true, also return reply comments

name: getConfluenceCommentChildren
description: Get reply(child) comments for a comment

contentFormat.description: Content format for body content. Use "markdown" for simplified plain text. Use "adf" (Atlassian Document Format, JSON) for full programmatic fidelity. Defaults vary by tool when omitted.

name: getConfluencePageDescendants
description: Get child pages of specified page

pageId.description: Page or blog post ID, or a Confluence tiny link ID (the encoded part from /wiki/x/ URLs, e.g., Fc1bBw)

name: createConfluencePage
description: Create a Confluence page or blog post

spaceId.description: Numeric space ID (a Long, e.g. "131073"). A space key (e.g. "ENG") or personal-space key (e.g. "~712020abc") is also accepted and resolved to the numeric ID automatically.

contentType.description: Type of content: page or blog post

parentId.description: Parent page ID (pages only, ignored for blogs)

body.description:
Page/comment body. When contentFormat is "html", use standard HTML for headings (

-

), paragraphs (

), tables (////
/), links (), code (
), and lists (
    /
      /
    1. ). Confluence-specific HTML+ uses data-type attributes, not CSS classes: panels 
      text
      ; status Label; task list 
      •  Item
      ; decision list 
      • text
      ; expand 
      Title
      content
      ; layout 
      Column
      ; inline card       text; date label; mention @Name; inline placeholder prompt; macro/extension 
      ; sync block 
      ; media group 
      file.pdf
      ; inline media file.pdf. For native Confluence macros use data-extension-type="com.atlassian.confluence.macro.core" and never storage XML (). Omit macroId. Do not invent opaque IDs: only copy data-user-id, data-id, data-collection, data-media-id, data-media-collection, data-resource-id, and data-local-id from existing content or tool output; omit data-local-id on new nodes. Preserve existing data-local-id when editing fetched HTML. Tables support data-number-column, data-layout="default|center|wide|full-width", data-display-mode="fixed", data-width, row/col spans, data-colwidth, and cell data-background. Breakout supports data-breakout="wide|full-width" on details, pre, section, and sync blocks. Follow ADF nesting: task/decision items, headings, and captions are inline-only; list items cannot contain headings/tables/panels/expands; panels cannot contain tables/expands/blockquotes/panels; table cells can contain headings, panels, lists, media, blockquote, decision list, and nested expand (
      ) but not nested tables or normal expand. Never wrap content in , , or  tags.

contentFormat.description: Content format for page/comment body. Use "html" to write or read Confluence content as structured HTML. HTML supports standard blocks plus Confluence HTML+ data-type nodes: panels, status, task/decision lists, expands, layouts, cards, media, dates, mentions, macros/extensions, and sync blocks. HTML is round-trip safe and preserves inline comments and existing local IDs. It must follow valid ADF nesting rules: inline-only task/decision items, headings, and captions; no self-nesting panels, expands, tables, or blockquotes. Invalid HTML will be rejected with a descriptive error so you can retry. Use "markdown" for simplified plain text. Defaults vary by tool when omitted.

subtype.description: Page subtype (pages only, ignored for blogs)

name: updateConfluencePage
description: Update a Confluence page or blog post

pageId.description: Page or blog post ID, or a Confluence tiny link ID (the encoded part from /wiki/x/ URLs, e.g., Fc1bBw)

contentType.description: Type of content: page or blog post

parentId.description: Parent page ID (pages only, ignored for blogs)

includeBody.description: If true, include the page body in the response; if false, omit it to reduce payload size.

name: createConfluenceFooterComment
description: Create a footer comment on a page or blog post

pageId.description: Page or blog post ID, or a Confluence tiny link ID (the encoded part from /wiki/x/ URLs, e.g., Fc1bBw)

contentType.description: Type of content: page or blog post

name: createConfluenceInlineComment
description: Create an inline comment on specific text in a page or blog post. For top-level comments, provide pageId and inlineCommentProperties (text selection + match counts). For replies, provide parentCommentId only (no pageId or inlineCommentProperties). You must first fetch the page content (e.g. using getConfluencePage) to find the exact text and count how many times it appears. Incorrect or missing inlineCommentProperties can result in a 400 error from the Confluence API.

pageId.description: Page or blog post ID, or a Confluence tiny link ID (the encoded part from /wiki/x/ URLs, e.g., Fc1bBw). Use for top-level inline comments only; do not provide with parentCommentId.

contentType.description: Type of content: page or blog post

parentCommentId.description: Parent comment ID for replies. When provided, this creates a reply to an existing inline comment thread. Do not provide pageId or inlineCommentProperties.

body.description:
Page/comment body. When contentFormat is "html", use standard HTML for headings (

contentFormat.description: Content format for page/comment body. Use "html" to write or read Confluence content as structured HTML. HTML supports standard blocks plus Confluence HTML+ data-type nodes: panels, status, task/decision lists, expands, layouts, cards, media, dates, mentions, macros/extensions, and sync blocks. HTML is round-trip safe and preserves inline comments and existing local IDs. It must follow valid ADF nesting rules: inline-only task/decision items, headings, and captions; no self-nesting panels, expands, tables, or blockquotes. Invalid HTML will be rejected with a descriptive error so you can retry. Use "markdown" for simplified plain text. Defaults vary by tool when omitted.

textSelection.description: The exact text on the page to anchor the inline comment to. Must match the rendered page text exactly (case-sensitive).

textSelectionMatchCount.description: Total number of times the textSelection string appears on the page. Fetch the page content and count occurrences before calling this tool.

textSelectionMatchIndex.description: Zero-based index of the specific occurrence of textSelection to anchor the comment to. Must be less than textSelectionMatchCount.

inlineCommentProperties.description: Required for top-level inline comments (not replies). Provide textSelection, textSelectionMatchCount (total occurrences on the page), and textSelectionMatchIndex (0-based, must be < textSelectionMatchCount).

name: getJiraIssue
title: Get issue
description: Get issue details

issueIdOrKey.description: Issue ID or key (e.g., PROJ-123 or 10000)

fields.description: Issue fields to return. When omitted or empty, defaults to: summary, description, status, issuetype, priority, labels, components, assignee, reporter, created, updated, resolution, project. Pass "*all" to return every field (including custom fields). Include "comment" to fetch issue comments in fields.comment.comments.

fieldsByKeys.description: Whether fields in the request are referenced by field keys instead of IDs.

expand.description: Additional issue details to expand in the response (for example, renderedFields or names).

properties.description: Issue property keys to include in the response.

updateHistory.description: Whether to update the user's recently viewed projects/history for this issue request.

failFast.description: Whether to fail quickly when some fields cannot be loaded instead of partially returning data.

responseContentFormat.description: Content format for body content. Use "markdown" for simplified plain text. Use "adf" (Atlassian Document Format, JSON) for full programmatic fidelity. Defaults vary by tool when omitted.

name: editJiraIssue
title: Update issue
description: Update issue. After updating, returns the issue using the same default read fields as getJiraIssue (summary, description, status, issuetype, priority, labels, components, assignee, reporter, created, updated, resolution, project). Use getJiraIssue with fields ["*all"] or a custom list if you need more.

issueIdOrKey.description: Issue ID or key (e.g., PROJ-123 or 10000)

fields.description: Fields to set, keyed by field name or customfield_* ID. To CLEAR a field, pass an explicit null, e.g. { "resolution": null }. Clearing the resolution is the fix when a reopened issue is blocked from transitioning because it still has a Resolution set.

contentFormat.description: Content format for body content. Use "markdown" for simplified plain text. Use "adf" (Atlassian Document Format, JSON) for full programmatic fidelity. Defaults vary by tool when omitted.

responseContentFormat.description: Content format for body content. Use "markdown" for simplified plain text. Use "adf" (Atlassian Document Format, JSON) for full programmatic fidelity. Defaults vary by tool when omitted.

name: createJiraIssue
title: Create issue
description: Create a Jira issue. Use additional_fields to set any Jira field that does not have its own parameter (e.g. custom fields, priority, components).

description.description: Issue description. With contentFormat "markdown" (default), pass plain text or Markdown as a string. With contentFormat "adf", pass an ADF JSON string or a document object with type "doc".

additional_fields.description: REQUIRED for custom fields. This is the ONLY parameter to set priority, labels, custom fields, components, fix versions, or any other Jira fields not listed above. Pass a JSON object with field names as keys. Examples: {"components": [{"name": "Backend"}]}, {"fixVersions": [{"name": "v1.2"}]}, {"priority": {"name": "High"}}, {"labels": ["bug"]}, {"customfield_10001": "value"}.

id.description: The workflow transition ID to apply during issue creation

transition.description: Optional workflow transition to apply during creation. Use getTransitionsForJiraIssue to find valid transition IDs. Placed at top level of Jira API request, not inside fields.

contentFormat.description: Content format for body content. Use "markdown" for simplified plain text. Use "adf" (Atlassian Document Format, JSON) for full programmatic fidelity. Defaults vary by tool when omitted.

responseContentFormat.description: Content format for body content. Use "markdown" for simplified plain text. Use "adf" (Atlassian Document Format, JSON) for full programmatic fidelity. Defaults vary by tool when omitted.

name: getTransitionsForJiraIssue
description: Get transitions

issueIdOrKey.description: Issue ID or key (e.g., PROJ-123 or 10000)

name: getJiraIssueRemoteIssueLinks
description: Get remote links

issueIdOrKey.description: Issue ID or key (e.g., PROJ-123 or 10000)

name: getVisibleJiraProjects
description: Get projects

name: getJiraProjectIssueTypesMetadata
description: Get issue types

name: getJiraIssueTypeMetaWithFields
description: Get field metadata

requiredFieldsOnly.description: When true (default), returns only the fields that are required to create an issue of this type, reducing response size. Set to false to return all available fields (both required and optional).

name: addCommentToJiraIssue
description: Add or update a comment on a Jira issue

issueIdOrKey.description: Issue ID or key (e.g., PROJ-123 or 10000)

contentFormat.description: Content format for body content. Use "markdown" for simplified plain text. Use "adf" (Atlassian Document Format, JSON) for full programmatic fidelity. Defaults vary by tool when omitted.

responseContentFormat.description: Content format for body content. Use "markdown" for simplified plain text. Use "adf" (Atlassian Document Format, JSON) for full programmatic fidelity. Defaults vary by tool when omitted.

commentId.description: ID of an existing comment to update. If omitted, a new comment is added.

name: transitionJiraIssue
title: Transition issue
description: Transition issue status

issueIdOrKey.description: Issue ID or key (e.g., PROJ-123 or 10000)

name: searchJiraIssuesUsingJql
title: Search with JQL
description: Search issues with JQL, total counts only when explicitly requested.

fields.description: Issue fields to return. When omitted or empty, defaults to: summary, description, status, issuetype, priority, labels, components, assignee, reporter, created, updated, resolution, project. Pass "*all" to return every field (including custom fields). Include "comment" to fetch issue comments in fields.comment.comments.

responseContentFormat.description: Content format for body content. Use "markdown" for simplified plain text. Use "adf" (Atlassian Document Format, JSON) for full programmatic fidelity. Defaults vary by tool when omitted.

computeIssueCount.description: Default false. Never set for normal search or if nextPageToken used. If total for same query is known, reuse it. Set true only when no trusted count exists, and either requested explicitly by user or downstream step cannot proceed without it.

name: lookupJiraAccountId
description: Lookup user IDs

name: addWorklogToJiraIssue
description: Add or update a worklog on a Jira issue. When worklogId is provided, updates that worklog;

issueIdOrKey.description: Issue ID or key (e.g., PROJ-123 or 10000)

timeSpent.description: Time spent (e.g. 2h, 30m, 4d). Required for new worklogs; use to set or change time when updating.

worklogId.description: ID of an existing worklog to update. If omitted, a new worklog is created.

started.description: When the work was started (ISO 8601 date-time, e.g. 2026-03-09T09:00:00.000+0000). If omitted, the worklog is booked at the current date/time.

contentFormat.description: Content format for body content. Use "markdown" for simplified plain text. Use "adf" (Atlassian Document Format, JSON) for full programmatic fidelity. Defaults vary by tool when omitted.

name: getIssueLinkTypes
description: Get available Jira issue link types (e.g. Blocks, Duplicate, Clones, Relates). For createIssueLink: inwardIssue = blocker, outwardIssue = blocked (e.g. "A is blocked by B" → inwardIssue: B, outwardIssue: A).

name: createIssueLink
description: Create a link between two Jira issues. For directional link types (e.g. Blocks): inwardIssue = issue that blocks, outwardIssue = issue that is blocked (e.g. "A is blocked by B" → inwardIssue: B, outwardIssue: A). Use getIssueLinkTypes if link type is unknown.

type.description: Link type name (e.g. Duplicate, Blocks, Clones, Relates). Use getIssueLinkTypes to list available types.

comment.description: Optional comment on the outward issue.

contentFormat.description: Content format for body content. Use "markdown" for simplified plain text. Use "adf" (Atlassian Document Format, JSON) for full programmatic fidelity. Defaults vary by tool when omitted.

name: getCompassComponents
description: Get a list of Compass components

query.description: The query to search for components.

repositories.description: Filter by repository URLs (e.g., ["https://bitbucket.org/workspace/repo-name"]). Use this filter to reverse look up a component for a given repository.

filters.description: Additional filters to narrow down the search. Results are returned for all the filters matched

maxResults.description: The maximum number of items to return per page.

name: getCompassComponent
description: Get a Compass component by ID

componentId.description: The ID of the component to get

includeCustomFieldsInResponse.description: Whether to include custom fields in the response. Components often track important information in custom fields

includeRelatedLinksInResponse.description: Whether to include related links to the Component in the response. Components often track links to repository, documentation, Jira project, operational dashboards, on-call schedule etc with links

includeRelatedComponentsAndDependenciesI.description: Whether to include related components and dependencies in the response. This includes all relationships (dependencies, child components, parent components, etc.) that this component has with other components

name: getCompassCustomFieldDefinitions
description: Get a list of Compass custom field definitions

name: createCompassCustomFieldDefinition
description: Create a custom field definition in Compass

name.description: Name of the custom field definition

description.description: Description of the custom field definition

isRequired.description: Whether the custom field is required

input.description: Input data for creating the custom field definition

name: createCompassComponent
description: Create a new Compass component

name: createCompassComponentRelationship
description: Create a relationship between Compass components

fromComponentId.description: The ARI of the source component

toComponentId.description: The ARI of the target component

name: getTeamworkGraphContext
description:
Retrieves connected context from Teamwork Graph for any Atlassian entity. Returns all relationships and linked objects within one traversal – including cross-product and third-party connections. Use when the answer requires connections between entities, not just a single entity's fields.

Supported entry points:

Jira: issues, projects, sprints, versions, comments

Confluence: pages, blogposts, whiteboards, databases, spaces

Goals, Projects, Focus Areas, Tags and updates

People: users, teams, organisations

DevOps: PRs, repos, deployments, services, builds, designs

Loom: videos, meetings

Compass: components

Assets: objects

Incidents, conversations, calendar events, external documents

After calling this tool, call getTeamworkGraphObject on returned ARIs to get full content.

objectType.description: Type of object to fetch context for.

objectIdentifier.description: Identifier for the object. It could be a key (e.g. ENG-1234), ID, ARI, or full URL; format depends on objectType.

relationshipTypes.description: Filter full results to specific relationship types to save tokens (e.g. 'jira-work-item-links-external-build' for Jira work items).

targetObjectTypes.description: Filter results to only include relationships targeting specific object types (e.g. AtlassianUser, ConfluenceComment).

from.description: ISO 8601 date-time string for the start of the time range (e.g. "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z").

to.description: ISO 8601 date-time string for the end of the time range (e.g. "2025-12-31T23:59:59Z").

timeRange.description: Optional time range to filter relationships by creation or update time.

first.description: Max number of related items to return.

after.description: Pagination cursor for fetching the next page of results.

name: getTeamworkGraphObject
description: Fetches the entire available data for one or more objects (Atlassian or third-party) using their ARIs or URLs. Use for the objects gathered from getTeamworkGraphContext tool.

objects.description: A list of object identifiers to hydrate. Supports both ARIs (e.g., 'ari:cloud:jira::issue/123') and full browser URLs (e.g., 'https://mysite.atlassian.net/browse/PROJ-1').

name: addTeamworkGraphContext
description: Adds a relationship between two entities in the Teamwork Graph (e.g. linking two Jira work items, marking one as blocking another, attaching a remote link, or connecting a Jira work item to an Atlas project or goal).

relationshipType.description: Type of relationship to create. Determines which entity types 'objectIdentifier' and 'targetObjectIdentifier' must resolve to.

objectIdentifier.description: Identifier of the source entity. Accepts an ARI, a full URL, or a stable key (e.g. ENG-123 for a Jira work item). Format depends on relationshipType.

targetObjectIdentifier.description: Identifier of the target entity. Accepts an ARI, a full URL, or a stable key (e.g. ATLAS-20426 for an Atlas project). Format depends on relationshipType.

title.description: Optional display label for the target. Used by relationships that surface a title (e.g. remote links); ignored otherwise.

name: search
description: Search Jira and Confluence using Rovo Search, ALWAYS use this tool to search for Jira and Confluence content unless the word CQL or JQL is used in the context

cloudId.description: Not needed for this tool — cloudId is derived from your access token automatically

query.description: The search query to use for Rovo Search

name: fetch
description: Get details of a Jira issue or Confluence page by ARI (Atlassian Resource Identifier), if the id is not an ARI, then use a different tool to fetch the content

cloudId.description: Not needed for this tool — cloudId is extracted from the ARI automatically

id.description: Atlassian Resource Identifier (ARI) from search results, e.g., "ari:cloud:jira:cloudId:issue/10107" or "ari:cloud:confluence:cloudId:page/123456789"
